code:
cumulative_sales = df.groupby(df['Date'].dt.year)['Total Sales'].sum().cumsum()
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.bar(cumulative_sales.index, cumulative_sales.values, color='skyblue')
plt.title('Cumulative Sales Per Year')
plt.xlabel('Year')
plt.ylabel('Cumulative Sales ($)')
plt.xticks(cumulative_sales.index)
plt.grid(axis='y')
plt.savefig('cumulative_sales_per_year.png')
plt.close()
result: A bar chart showing the cumulative sales per year from 2022 to 2024.

question: what is the difference in terms of percentage between the lowest and the highest
code:
highest_sales = df.groupby('Product')['Total Sales'].sum().idxmax()
highest_amount = df.groupby('Product')['Total Sales'].sum().max()

lowest_sales = df.groupby('Product')['Total Sales'].sum().idxmin()
lowest_amount = df.groupby('Product')['Total Sales'].sum().min()

difference_percentage = ((highest_amount - lowest_amount) / lowest_amount) * 100
result = {'product': highest_sales, 'amount': highest_amount, 'lowest_product': lowest_sales, 'lowest_amount': lowest_amount, 'difference_percentage': difference_percentage}
